title: Prefetch, Goroutine Concurrency, and Capacity · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs prefetch, goroutine concurrency, and capacity from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a measured consumer-capacity budget after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Prefetch Window Semantics · changed case · Review Prefetch Window Semantics
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 1827 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine prefetch, active goroutines, dependency capacity, and message-byte budgets; reject stale connection and channel generations; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Prefetch Window Semantics without dropping earlier contracts?
